key_insight: A dual-continuum phase-field model separately tracks fracture and matrix pressures so viscosity-dominated hydraulic fracturing and fluid lag can be modeled without smearing the pressure field.
engineering_relevance: This is important for realistic subsurface injection design because high-rate fracturing often operates in a viscosity-dominated regime where fracture pressure drops and lag strongly affect propagation.
method: Couples a phase-field fracture model with double-porosity microporomechanics.
Uses two independent fluid pressures: mesoscale crack pressure and micropore pressure.
Modifies a fixed-stress split and adds a variational inequality to stabilize coupling and enforce fluid lag.
evidence_signal: The model is verified against closed-form solutions in toughness-dominated, viscosity-dominated, and early-time transitional regimes and reproduces transient fluid lag.
watch_item: The added dual-pressure coupling and inequality constraint may increase implementation complexity and require careful numerical stabilization.
paper_title: A dual--continuum phase-field model for hydraulic fracturing: Viscosity-dominated regime and fluid lag
